title: viola canina subsp. lancifolia
intro: Viola canina subsp. lancifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved dog violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the viola genus. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Viola canina subsp. lancifolia include its ability to soothe digestive issues and alleviate menstrual cramps due to its anthocyanin content, which has antioxidant properties. Viola canina subsp. lancifolia is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in a carrier oil or water.
